pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 62, 75: 'if tris > 10000:' checks are >10000, not >=10000. Also contradicted by min_file_size_bytes.

pi_items.verified_value: >200000 (per verifier); mathematically incompatible with min_triangle_count claim
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 108-109: checks '> 200000'. Binary STL format: 84 bytes header + 50 bytes per triangle. 204800 bytes = ~4094 triangles max, not 10000.

pi_items.verified_value: ct_cranium directory with 0051.zip contents (50 DICOM slices, ~26.4MB total)
pi_items.source: setup_invesalius.sh line 72-73: https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. Verified by downloading and inspecting contents. Real CT data from InVesalius v3.0 official release.
Confirmed as standard InVesalius bone preset via GitHub invesalius/invesalius3 documentation

pi_items.verified_value: Adult skin: -718 to -177 HU; Child skin: -766 to -202 HU

privileged_info_summary: The task involves exporting two STL models from a CT Cranium scan (real DICOM data from InVesalius v3.0 official release). The bone model should use the 226-3071 HU preset, and the skin model should use the skin/soft tissue presets (-718 to -177 HU for adults). Outputs must be valid binary STL files at /home/ga/Documents/bone_model.stl and /home/ga/Documents/skin_model.stl. The verifier requires both files to have >10000 triangles and >200000 bytes (200 KB). NOTE: The metadata claims min_file_size_bytes of 204800 bytes is mathematically incompatible with min_triangle_count of 10000 triangles (204800 bytes can only hold ~4094 triangles). The actual verification uses >10000 and >200000 bytes thresholds.
